quiz.title: Anita and the Honeybees - Chapter 5 Quiz

questions.explanation: Honeybees live together in a hive. A hive is their home where they make honey and raise their young.

questions.questionText: What do bees collect from flowers?
questions.explanation: Bees collect nectar from flowers. They use nectar to make honey.
hints: It's a sweet liquid in flowers

questions.questionText: The leader of a bee colony is called the:
questions.explanation: The leader of a bee colony is the Queen bee. She lays all the eggs in the hive.

questions.questionText: True or False: Bees help flowers by carrying pollen from one flower to another.
questions.explanation: True! Bees carry pollen between flowers. This is called pollination and helps plants make seeds and fruits.

questions.questionText: Worker bees in a hive are:
questions.explanation: Worker bees are all female. They do all the work - collecting nectar, making honey, and protecting the hive.
hints: There are thousands of them doing all the work

questions.questionText: Honey is stored in the hive in:
questions.explanation: Honey is stored in honeycomb cells - small hexagonal (six-sided) wax chambers made by bees.
Bees do a special dance (called waggle dance) to tell other bees where to find good flowers with nectar!

questions.questionText: True or False: Bees can sting only once and then they die.
questions.explanation: True for honeybees! When a honeybee stings, its stinger gets stuck and the bee dies. This is why they only sting when threatened.
Beekeeping is called apiculture. 'Api' comes from the Latin word for bee (apis).

questions.questionText: Why are bees important for farmers?
questions.explanation: Bees are important because they pollinate crops. Without bees, many fruits and vegetables wouldn't grow!
hints: Think about what bees do with flowers
